But in C++, the code would be:

class Solution {
public:
    int maximumLength(string s) {
        // Process runs
        map<char, vector<int>> runLengths;
        if (s.empty()) return -1; // but per constraints, s has at least 3 chars
        char currentChar = s[0];
        int currentCount = 1;
        for (int i=1; i < s.length(); ++i) {
            if (s[i] == currentChar) {
                currentCount++;
            } else {
                runLengths[currentChar].push_back(currentCount);
                currentChar = s[i];
                currentCount = 1;
            }
        }
        runLengths[currentChar].push_back(currentCount);
        
        // Now build countMap
        map<char, map<int, int>> countMap;
        for (auto & [c, runs] : runLengths) {
            for (int runIdx = 0; runIdx < runs.size(); ++runIdx) {
                int L = runs[runIdx];
                for (int k = 1; k <= L; ++k) {
                    int contribution = L - k + 1;
                    countMap[c][k] += contribution;
                }
            }
        }
        
        // Now find the maximum k where countMap[c][k] >=3
        int maxLen = -1;
        for (auto & [c, kMap] : countMap) {
            for (auto & [k, count] : kMap) {
                if (count >=3) {
                    if (k > maxLen) {
                        maxLen = k;
                    }
                }
            }
        }
        return maxLen != -1 ? maxLen : -1;
    }
};

class Solution {
public:
    int maximumLength(string s) {
        // Process runs
        map<char, vector<int>> runLengths;
        if (s.empty()) return -1; // but per constraints, s has at least 3 chars
        char currentChar = s[0];
        int currentCount = 1;
        for (int i=1; i < s.length(); ++i) {
            if (s[i] == currentChar) {
                currentCount++;
            } else {
                runLengths[currentChar].push_back(currentCount);
                currentChar = s[i];
                currentCount = 1;
            }
        }
        runLengths[currentChar].push_back(currentCount);
        
        // Now build countMap
        map<char, map<int, int>> countMap;
        for (auto & [c, runs] : runLengths) {
            for (int runIdx = 0; runIdx < runs.size(); ++runIdx) {
                int L = runs[runIdx];
                for (int k = 1; k <= L; ++k) {
                    int contribution = L - k + 1;
                    countMap[c][k] += contribution;
                }
            }
        }
        
        // Now find the maximum k where countMap[c][k] >=3
        int maxLen = -1;
        for (auto & [c, kMap] : countMap) {
            for (auto & [k, count] : kMap) {
                if (count >=3) {
                    if (k > maxLen) {
                        maxLen = k;
                    }
                }
            }
        }
        return maxLen != -1 ? maxLen : -1;
    }
};

solution: 
```cpp
class Solution {
public:
    int maximumLength(string s) {
        std::map<char, std::vector<int>> runLengths;
        if (s.empty()) return -1;
        char currentChar = s[0];
        int currentCount = 1;
        for (int i = 1; i < s.length(); ++i) {
            if (s[i] == currentChar) {
                ++currentCount;
            } else {
                runLengths[currentChar].push_back(currentCount);
                currentChar = s[i];
                currentCount = 1;
            }
        }
        runLengths[currentChar].push_back(currentCount);
        
        std::map<char, std::map<int, int>> countMap;
        for (auto& [c, runs] : runLengths) {
            for (int runIdx = 0; runIdx < runs.size(); ++runIdx) {
                int L = runs[runIdx];
                for (int k = 1; k <= L; ++k) {
                    int contribution = L - k + 1;
                    countMap[c][k] += contribution;
                }
            }
        }
        
        int maxLen = -1;
        for (auto& [c, kMap] : countMap) {
            for (auto& [k, count] : kMap) {
                if (count >= 3 && k > maxLen) {
                    maxLen = k;
                }
            }
        }
        return maxLen != -1 ? maxLen : -1;
    }
};
```
efficiency: HIGH
time complexity: O(n^2)
space complexity: O(n^2)
energy implications: LOW
explanation: The solution processes the input string to identify runs of consecutive characters. For each run, it calculates contributions to all possible special substring lengths and tracks their occurrences. It then determines the maximum length of a special substring that appears at least three times. The approach efficiently handles all possible substrings within runs, leveraging nested maps for counting and ensuring optimal performance for small input sizes.
